title: Rovibrational structure and electric dipole moments of the AcOCH$_3$+ ion
abstract: The possibility of laser cooling and the presence of closely spaced rovibrational doublets make polyatomic molecules an attractive platform for the $\mathcal{P}$, $\mathcal{T}$-violation searches. We study the spectrum of the lowest rovibrational state of the AcOCH$_3+$ symmetric top molecule. The electronic structure full-electron computation was performed within a relativistic coupled cluster method with double and perturbative triple excitations. The rovibrational wavefunctions are obtained using a coupled channel technique, taking into account all rovibrational effects and anharmonicities of the potential. As a result, the vibrational frequencies, as well as the values of the electric dipole moments for the rovibrational states, were computed.
summary: This paper provides a detailed theoretical calculation of the rovibrational spectrum and electric dipole moments of the AcOCH3+ ion. By using relativistic coupled cluster methods and a coupled channel technique, the study identifies key molecular properties essential for future experiments searching for parity and time-reversal symmetry violation.
main_problem: The study aims to investigate the rovibrational structure and electric dipole moments of the AcOCH3+ ion to support searches for fundamental parity (P) and time-reversal (T) symmetry violation.
main_result: The researchers computed vibrational frequencies and longitudinal electric dipole moments for various rovibrational states, finding that the longitudinal dipole moment behaves nearly linearly with the internuclear distance.
method: Electronic structure was calculated using the relativistic coupled cluster method [CCSD(T)] via the DIRAC 19 program, while nuclear motion was treated using a coupled channel technique to account for all rovibrational effects and anharmonicities.
model_or_system: The system is the AcOCH3+ molecular ion, modeled as a symmetric top molecule consisting of a linear rotor (Actinium atom and the center of mass of the OCH3 ligand) coupled to a rigid symmetric top representing the ligand.
key_observables: Vibrational frequencies, longitudinal electric dipole moments (d_parallel), and energy levels (E - E0).
important_parameters: l-doublets, K-doublets, equilibrium distance (Re), vibrational frequency (omega), and dissociation energy (De).
main_assumptions: The variation of the potential with the angle phi is treated as a perturbation for most properties, and the radial coordinate is discretized using a harmonic approximation.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows the molecular geometry; Figure 2 shows the adiabatic potential dependence on R; Figure 3 shows the phi-averaged adiabatic potential dependence on theta; Figure 4 shows the azimuthal dependence of the potential and wavefunction extent.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the motivation for P,T-violation searches, describes the molecular model and Hamiltonian, details the relativistic electronic structure and coupled channel methods, presents the calculated energy levels and dipole moments, and concludes with the results.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to AMO physics as it provides the necessary molecular spectroscopic data (dipole moments and energy levels) required to implement laser cooling and precision spectroscopy for testing fundamental symmetries.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 2: The dependence of the adiabatic potential (in cm−1) for θ = 0◦on R (in a.u.) The ground state and first longitudinally excited state energy levels are depicted as horizontal lines.

Figure 3: The dependence of the ϕ-averaged adiabatic potential (in cm−1) for R = Req on θ (in Degrees). The ground state and the lowest transversely excited state energy levels are depicted as horizontal lines.
Figure 4: The dependence of the adiabatic potential (in cm−1) for θ = 10◦on ϕ (in ◦) For comparison the distance between the ground state and first transversely excited energy level is depicted.
